title: Shadows over Phandalin
subtitle: Family-friendly starter, prepared sessions and a living town
description: Frontier sample campaign designed to teach session prep, clear clues, memorable NPCs, town consequences and the player portal.
summary: Phandalin is a frontier town built over old ruins, unsafe roads and mining promises. Gundren’s disappearance pushes the party toward goblins, bandits, frightened neighbors and a threat still operating from afar.
recommendedFor: DMs who want to try DMCC with an easy-to-run table campaign, ideal for families or new groups: prepared scenes, clear goals, public material for the player portal and enough secrets to practice revelations without overwhelming the table.
pitch: A starter campaign for learning DMCC through a comfortable structure: central town, clear quests, memorable NPCs, visible clues and consequences that make players feel Phandalin changing with their decisions.
learningGoals: Prepare classic adventure sessions with goals, clues and a short checklist.
Use the town as a living hub where each NPC can react to the party’s actions.
Separate public clues, DM secrets and consequences that change Phandalin’s tone.
Publish safe player-facing information without revealing the whole mystery.
includedMaterial: Five prepared sessions from the opening ambush to the lost mine.
Town NPCs, factions, locations, clues, rumors and table handouts.
Consequence arcs for freeing Phandalin or letting fear grow.
A threat canvas for reading Redbrands, Gundren and the Black Spider at a glance.
Narrative pressure guidance and dramatic questions for all 134 entities
Complete operational notes for all 8 prepared sessions

quickStart.title: How to start Shadows over Phandalin
steps: Open the fronts canvas and find three axes: missing Gundren, Redbrands in town and the mine as a future promise.
Run session 1 as a simple adventure: ambush, clear clue and a choice to follow tracks or protect supplies.
In session 2, use town NPCs so every player has someone to talk to.
When the party acts against the Redbrands, record how Phandalin’s mood changes as a consequence.
Before play, review the featured relationships and choose which secret can advance without being fully revealed.
After each session, update states, connections, and consequences instead of recording only a chronicle.

ent_tpl_phandalin_note.title: How to use Phandalin in DMCC
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.summary: This example teaches DMCC’s most direct flow: prepare a session, run clear scenes, record clues and let the town react to the party.
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.content:
This template is designed for family tables: clear goals, recognizable NPCs and simple decisions with visible consequences.
## Table use
Use this as an operational guide: after every session record what changed, who knows, and which consequence is now ready.
## Narrative pressure
Which campaign element must be updated after the next session?

metadata.dramaticQuestion: Which campaign element must be updated after the next session?
metadata.defaultApproach: Use this as an operational guide: after every session record what changed, who knows, and which consequence is now ready.

ent_tpl_phandalin_town.title: Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.subtitle: Frontier town
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.summary: A small settlement trying to prosper while living under external threats and internal intimidation.
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.content:
Phandalin was resettled a generation ago over the bones of an older town — cellars, wells and cracked foundations still surface when someone digs too deep. Most families arrived chasing cheap land near the old dwarven road; what they found instead was a town too small to police itself and too stubborn to leave.
## Table use
Treat the place as an active situation rather than scenery. Show what changed since the last visit and who benefits from that change.

ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.content:
Triboar Trail carries most of the traffic between Phandalin and the Sword Coast, which makes it worth watching. Goblins from Cragmaw have grown bold enough to strike within sight of the crossroads, banking on the fact that nobody in Phandalin has the manpower to chase them into the woods.

ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.title: Cragmaw Hideout
ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.summary: A goblin cave that teaches the group Gundren’s disappearance was not random.
ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.content:
A shallow cave system with a stream running through it, chosen by the Cragmaw because the water muffles footsteps and drowns torchlight before it can warn anyone deeper in. Klarg holds it through fear and stolen loot, not loyalty — exactly the crack the party can use.

ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.summary: Ruins hiding the Redbrand base and several moral choices for the group.
ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.content:
Once the seat of a minor noble family, Tresendar Manor burned decades ago and was never rebuilt above ground. Below it, the old cellars survived intact: cool, dry and forgotten by everyone except the people using them as a base now.

ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.content:
Wave Echo Cave was sealed after an earlier expedition ended in disaster, and the stories that leaked out afterward kept most treasure hunters away for a generation. What's left inside is worth more than gold: a working forge older than Phandalin itself.

ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.summary: He hired the party to escort supplies. His map is the piece everyone wants.
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.content:
Gundren talks fast and trusts easily, which is either his best quality or the reason his brothers keep bailing him out. He fronted the coin for this expedition on the strength of a map he wouldn't fully explain, even to Sildar.

ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.content:

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.content:

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.content:
Iarno kept the wizard persona alive because it let him move through town without suspicion, buying supplies and asking questions no one thought twice about. The moment that mask slips, every favor he's called in becomes a liability.

ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.summary: If the Redbrands fall, the town starts acting, speaking and asking for help in visible ways.
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.content:
Confidence returns slowly and unevenly — not everyone speaks up at once. Give a couple of scenes to specific NPCs finding their nerve before treating the whole town as safe.
## Table use

fact_tpl_phandalin_1.statement: Gundren disappeared before the supplies reached Phandalin.

fact_tpl_phandalin_2.statement: The Redbrands control the town’s fear more than its physical territory.

fact_tpl_phandalin_3.statement: Iarno and Glasstaff are the same person.

fact_tpl_phandalin_4.statement: Wave Echo Cave is not only a mine: it contains a coveted source of magical power.

fact_tpl_phandalin_5.statement: If the party saves Phandalin from the Redbrands, neighbors will start offering information without interrogation.

fact_tpl_phandalin_6.statement: Toblen knows which neighbors pay the Redbrands, but needs to see that the party can protect specific people.

fact_tpl_phandalin_7.statement: Halia can be a temporary ally against the Redbrands, though she will try to fill the power vacuum they leave.

fact_tpl_phandalin_8.statement: The black-seal note proves Glasstaff is under pressure from someone interested in the mine.

fact_tpl_phandalin_9.statement: Gundren’s map works best if handed out incomplete: the table gets a clear promise without skipping the town arc.

fact_tpl_phandalin_10.statement: If the characters resolve a public threat without humiliating the town, neighbors will begin offering voluntary help.

fact_tpl_phandalin_11.statement: Qelline has no political power, but she does have a trusted network that avoids unnecessary fights.

fact_tpl_phandalin_12.statement: Nezznar prefers adventurers wasting time on local trouble before they look toward Wave Echo Cave.

fact_tpl_phandalin_13.statement: Gundren's map works as a campaign clock: every delay increases the chance it changes hands.

fact_tpl_phandalin_14.statement: Vyerith can turn a victory at Cragmaw Castle into a chase if she steals or replaces key information.

fact_tpl_phandalin_15.statement: Tharden Rockseeker has already paid the price of Wave Echo, preventing the ending from being just a race for loot.

fact_tpl_phandalin_16.statement: Wave Echo Cave holds a real magical source: deteriorated but still powerful.

fact_tpl_phandalin_17.statement: Hamun Kost is a lateral threat or an uncomfortable source, not an automatic extension of the Black Spider.

fact_tpl_phandalin_18.statement: Venomfang should appear as a negotiating, intelligent danger; the table should understand that not every challenge is meant to be won by force.

fact_tpl_phandalin_19.statement: Defeating the Redbrands and the Black Spider raises a political question: who protects Phandalin when the adventurers leave?

fact_tpl_phandalin_20.statement: The next prepared session should open with a clear decision: enter Tresendar, seek more local support or pursue the Cragmaw trail to the castle.

prep.summary: Direct opening: attack, trail, rescue and first emotional link to Gundren’s disappearance.
prep.openingPrompt: The road narrows between trees. Two horses block the path. They do not neigh. They do not move. And the arrows are already ready.
goals: Teach the family-adventure tone.
Reveal that Gundren is in danger.
Offer a clear decision: follow the trail or protect the cargo.

chk_ph_1.label: Show the horses before the attack.

chk_ph_2.label: Give the group a visible clue toward the hideout.

prep.notes:
### Suggested pacing
Open with a visible consequence, place a decision before the first third, and end once the party has changed the situation even if the main objective remains open.

### Active cast and elements
- Gundren Rockseeker
- Sildar Hallwinter

### Available clues
- Dead horses on the road
Never lock these clues behind one roll: change their cost or precision instead.

### Session close
Record what changed, who knows it, which relationship became strained, and what should open the next session.

prep.summary: Present Phandalin as a living place and prepare the Redbrand arc.
prep.openingPrompt: Phandalin looks quiet until someone lowers their voice when a red cloak appears across the street.
goals: Connect players to specific neighbors.
Show the Redbrands as a nearby problem.
Offer several entrances toward Tresendar.

chk_ph_3.label: Make the town’s fear visible, not explained.
